title: linum hirsutum subsp. spathulatum
intro: Linum hirsutum subsp. spathulatum, commonly known as the sticky flax or hairy flax, is a subspecies of the Linum hirsutum plant, which is a species of flax that belongs to the family Linaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Linum hirsutum subsp. spathulatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable herb for traditional medicine. Linum hirsutum subsp. spathulatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
